<parameter=code>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After the second click on the same spot, the board seems to have reset to the original state! The center now has gray, gray, purple, gray (same as the first click but shifted). The bottom row is back to blue, green, gray, yellow. The top row is blue, green, yellow, purple.
Wait, looking more carefully. The first click: center was purple, gray, gray, gray. After click: white, purple, gray, gray. Second click: gray, gray, purple, gray.
So it seems like clicking on the center square cycles the colors. Let me try clicking on different squares to see if I click on the same square twice, it resets. Let me try clicking on a different square.
Actually wait - the first click was purple, gray, gray, gray. After click 1st click: white, purple, gray, gray. After 2nd click: gray, gray, purple, gray. So it seems like the purple moved right, and the gray squares shift left. The white appeared from nowhere.
Let me try clicking on a different square in the center box. Let me try clicking on the gray square on the right side of the center box.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model:
- Level 1: Center box starts as purple, gray, gray, gray. Clicking center causes color shift: purple moves right, gray shifts left, white appears.
- After 2 clicks on same spot: center goes purple, gray, gray, gray -> white, purple, gray, gray -> gray, gray, purple, gray.
- Goal: Need to figure out what matching pattern solves the puzzle.
- Plan: Try clicking on different squares in center box.
